96		// to dispatch a single specific bead through the same claim → executor →
97		// land path the queue drain uses. When empty, the picker behaves normally.
98		TargetBeadID string
99		// WakeCh, when non-nil, signals the idle-poll sleep to return immediately
100		// so the loop re-scans the queue. Used by the operator-prompt approve /
101		// auto-approve mutations (Story 15) to avoid an idle-interval-sized delay
102		// before a freshly-approved bead is claimed. Implementations must send
103		// non-blocking (server-side helpers do); the loop only waits for a
104		// receive on WakeCh during the idle sleep, never elsewhere.
105		WakeCh <-chan struct{}
106	
107		// OnAttemptFinalized, when non-nil, is called once per finalized attempt
108		// immediately after the report is appended to loop results. Best-effort:
109		// the hook must not block and its errors are silently discarded.
110		OnAttemptFinalized func(report ExecuteBeadReport)
111		// PostLadderExhaustionHook, when non-nil, is called when a bead's
112		// consecutive_ladder_exhaustions counter reaches the auto-recovery
113		// threshold (>= 2). A nil hook or Attempted=false result falls through to
114		// the existing loop path unchanged.
115		PostLadderExhaustionHook PostLadderExhaustionHook
116	}
117	
118	func (r ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime) loopIntent() (executeloop.Mode, time.Duration) {
119		mode := r.Mode
120		if mode == "" {
121			if r.Once {
122				mode = executeloop.ModeOnce
123			} else {
124				mode = executeloop.ModeDrain
125			}
126		}
127		idleInterval := r.IdleInterval
128		if idleInterval == 0 && mode == executeloop.ModeWatch {
129			idleInterval = r.PollInterval
130		}
131		if idleInterval == 0 && mode == executeloop.ModeWatch {
132			idleInterval = 30 * time.Second
133		}
134		return mode, idleInterval
135	}
136	
137	// DefaultReviewMaxRetries is the number of reviewer attempts allowed per
138	// committed result_rev before the loop emits a terminal
139	// `review-manual-required` event and stops re-executing primary. FEAT-022 §14.
140	const DefaultReviewMaxRetries = 3
141	
142	// ReviewerSkippedEmptyDiffEventKind is the event kind emitted when the
143	// reviewer dispatch is skipped because the implementer produced no commits
144	// (BaseRev == ResultRev). Per ADR-024 P1: cheapest first — paying a reviewer
145	// to evaluate an empty diff is unjustified cost.
146	const ReviewerSkippedEmptyDiffEventKind = "reviewer-skipped-empty-diff"
147	
148	// MaxLoopCooldown is the absolute upper bound the execute-loop will set for
149	// any execute-loop-retry-after value. Year-scale parks effectively mean
150	// "never retry" and that should be a deliberate operator choice via
151	// `ddx bead update --set execute-loop-retry-after=...`, not an automatic
152	// loop decision. Beyond this cap, the loop refuses to lengthen the cooldown
153	// further; an operator extending it manually still works.
154	const MaxLoopCooldown = 24 * time.Hour

(no operator-pinned provider/model). Three targeted unit tests cover payload
population, expiry filtering, and escalation on all-excluded routes.

# Lefthook configuration for DDx project
# Fast, cross-platform git hooks
# https://github.com/evilmartians/lefthook

# Configuration
colors: true
no_tty: false
assertion: false

# Reusable command groups
command_groups:
  go_checks: &go_checks
    root: "cli/"  # Go module root
    tags: [go, backend]

  security_checks: &security_checks
    tags: [security]

  formatting_checks: &formatting_checks
    tags: [format, style]

# ====================
# Pre-commit hooks - Fast checks on staged files
# ====================
pre-commit:
  parallel: true

  commands:
    # Conflict detection using grep on staged files only.
    # Uses git diff --cached directly to avoid {staged_files} tty/expansion issues
    # when running detached (e.g. ddx work --local, no controlling tty).
    conflicts:
      name: "Check merge conflicts"
      run: |
        files=$(git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR 2>/dev/null)
        [ -z "$files" ] && exit 0
        conflict_file=$(printf '%s\n' "$files" | while IFS= read -r f; do
          [ -f "$f" ] || continue
          grep -lE "^<<<<<<<[ 	]|^=======$|^>>>>>>>[ 	]|^\|{7}[ 	]" "$f" 2>/dev/null && break
        done | head -1)

population, expiry filtering, and escalation on all-excluded routes.

data.text:
All pre-commit checks pass and the commit succeeded. 

**Summary of changes**:

- **`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go`**: Added `RouteExclusionWindow` (1h constant), `buildExcludedRoutes` (converts `FailedRouteEntry` list to Fizeau `ExcludedRoute` values filtered by time window), and `CheckAndApplyRouteExclusions` (pre-dispatch check that calls `ResolveRoute` with the exclusions and escalates `TriagePowerHintKey` when no viable candidate remains).

- **`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_route_exclusion_test.go`**: Added the 3 required tests: `TestRouteRequest_PopulatedFromFailedRoutes`, `TestRouteRequest_ExpiredFailedRoutesDropped`, `TestRouteRequest_AllRoutesExcludedTriggersEscalation`.

- **`cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go`**: Wired `CheckAndApplyRouteExclusions` into the executor closure for free-routing attempts — calls `ResolveRoute` with the bead's recent failed routes as `ExcludedRoutes`, returns early with escalated `TriagePowerHintKey` if no viable candidate exists.
